title: "RMD1Ch2P2_trees"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-12"
---

## Load Packages 
```{r }

#https://hyphy.org/resources/json-fields.pdf
library(ape)
library(ggtree)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidytext)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)
library(scales)
library(glue)
library(tidytext)
library(ggtext)
library(KEGGREST)
#BiocManager::install("sangerseqR")
#devtools::install_version("bold", version="0.9.0", dependencies = FALSE)
#install.packages('datelife', repos = c('https://phylotastic.r-universe.dev', 'https://cloud.r-project.org'))
library(datelife)
library(cowplot)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)
library(ggrepel)
library(patchwork)
library(forcats)
library(scales)
library(tidyverse)
#install.packages("eulerr")
library(eulerr) 
#install.packages("UpSetR")
library(UpSetR)    
library(phylocomr)
# install.packages("rotl")
# install.packages("ape") 
# install.packages("devtools")
# install.packages("stringr")
# install.packages("datelife")
library(rotl)
library(ape)
library(devtools)
library(rlang)
library(ggpubr)
#install.packages("ggridges")
library(ggridges)
library(gridExtra)
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)


```





#Ortholog detect
```{r}
library(httr)
library(jsonlite)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(furrr)
# install.packages("RMariaDB")
# install.packages("DBI")
#install.packages("RMySQL")
library(RMySQL)
library(DBI)
library(RMariaDB)

output_dir <- "OrthologTester"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

#########ONE2ONE ORTHOLOG FILTER #######
manifest <- read_csv("manifest.csv")
genes <- unique(na.omit(manifest$gene))
con <- dbConnect(MySQL(), host = "ensembldb.ensembl.org", port = 3306,
                 user = "anonymous", dbname = "ensembl_compara_113")
species_set <- c("pan_troglodytes","pan_paniscus","gorilla_gorilla","pongo_abelii",
  "nomascus_leucogenys","chlorocebus_sabaeus","macaca_mulatta","macaca_fascicularis",
  "macaca_nemestrina","papio_anubis","callithrix_jacchus","saimiri_boliviensis_boliviensis",
  "aotus_nancymaae","microcebus_murinus","colobus_angolensis_palliatus","cercocebus_atys",
  "theropithecus_gelada","prolemur_simus","carlito_syrichta","propithecus_coquereli")
q <- sprintf("SELECT gm1.display_label AS gene, gdb2.name AS species, h.description AS homology_type
  FROM homology h JOIN homology_member hm1 ON h.homology_id=hm1.homology_id
  JOIN homology_member hm2 ON h.homology_id=hm2.homology_id AND hm1.gene_member_id<>hm2.gene_member_id
  JOIN gene_member gm1 ON hm1.gene_member_id=gm1.gene_member_id
  JOIN gene_member gm2 ON hm2.gene_member_id=gm2.gene_member_id
  JOIN genome_db gdb1 ON gm1.genome_db_id=gdb1.genome_db_id
  JOIN genome_db gdb2 ON gm2.genome_db_id=gdb2.genome_db_id
  WHERE gdb1.name='homo_sapiens' AND gdb2.name IN ('%s') AND gm1.display_label IN ('%s')
  AND h.description='ortholog_one2one'",
  paste(species_set, collapse="','"), paste(genes, collapse="','"))
tags <- dbGetQuery(con, q) %>% distinct()
dbDisconnect(con)

MIN_ONE2ONE_SPECIES <- 15

tagged <- manifest %>% left_join(tags, by = c("gene", "species")) %>%
  filter(species != "homo_sapiens" & status == "alternative")
tagged2 <- manifest %>% left_join(tags, by = c("gene", "species")) %>%
  filter(species != "homo_sapiens" & status == "canonical")
exclusively_one2one_genes <- tagged %>%
  group_by(gene) %>%
  filter( n_distinct(species[homology_type == "ortholog_one2one" & !is.na(homology_type)]) >= MIN_ONE2ONE_SPECIES ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  pull(gene) %>%
  unique() %>%
  sort()
exclusively_one2one_genesV2 <- tagged2 %>%
  group_by(gene) %>%
  filter( n_distinct(species[homology_type == "ortholog_one2one" & !is.na(homology_type)]) >= MIN_ONE2ONE_SPECIES ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  pull(gene) %>%
  unique() %>%
  sort()
one2one_filter <- c(exclusively_one2one_genesV2,exclusively_one2one_genes) %>% sort() %>% unique()
##############################################
  
length(one2one_filter)



filter_one2manyID <- function(df, gene_col) {
  df %>%
    mutate(.gene_tmp = toupper(.data[[gene_col]])) %>%
    filter(.gene_tmp %in% one2one_filter) %>%
    select(-.gene_tmp)
}

demons <- c(
  "ABAT", "ACOT2", "ADH1A", "ADH1B", "ADH1C", "ADH6", "APOA2", "ATP1A4", "CETP",
  "CH25H", "CTH", "CYP2A6", "CYP2A7", "CYP2C8", "CYP2C9", "CYP3A4", "CYP3A7",
  "DHFR2", "DHRS4L2", "GPAT2", "LDHA", "LDHAL6B", "LPA", "PGAM4", "PLA2G2C",
  "PLA2G4C", "PRKACG", "SLCO1B1", "UGT1A10", "UGT1A3", "UGT1A5", "UGT1A6",
  "UGT1A7", "UGT1A8", "UGT1A9", "UGT2A2", "UGT2B10", "UGT2B11", "UGT2B15",
  "UGT2B28", "UGT2B4", "UGT2B7"
)

exorcise <- function(df, gene_col) {
  df %>%
    mutate(.gene_tmp = toupper(.data[[gene_col]])) %>%
    filter(!.gene_tmp %in% demons) %>%
    select(-.gene_tmp)
}
 

```


## Canonical Species Tree

```{r, fig 1a}
####################. 
#. WE START WITH A TREE OF TIME DIVERGENCE TO DEMONSTRATE FOR READERS BROADLY ACCEPTED PHYLO PATTERNS
####################. 

species <- c("Homo sapiens",
             "Pan troglodytes",
             "Pan paniscus",
             "Gorilla gorilla",
             "Pongo abelii",
             "Nomascus leucogenys",
             "Chlorocebus sabaeus",
             "Macaca mulatta",
             "Macaca fascicularis",
             "Macaca nemestrina",
             "Papio anubis",
             "Callithrix jacchus",
             "Saimiri boliviensis",
             "Aotus nancymaae",
             "Microcebus murinus",
             "Colobus angolensis",
             "Cercocebus atys",
             "Theropithecus gelada",
             "Prolemur simus",
             "Carlito syrichta",
             "Propithecus coquereli") #%>% paste(., collapse = ",")

# ----------------------------
# 2. Query datelife # #for mac users
# ----------------------------
# message("Querying datelife for time-calibrated trees...")


# q <- make_datelife_query(species)
# dl <- datelife_search(input = q, summary_format = "phylo_median")


#For Windows users

# ----------------------------
# 2. Query OpenTree
# ----------------------------
matches <- tnrs_match_names(species)
tree <- tol_induced_subtree(ott_ids = matches$ott_id)

# ----------------------------
# 3. Remove OTT numbers (correct pattern)
# ----------------------------
tree$tip.label <- sub("_ott\\d+$", "", tree$tip.label)

# ----------------------------
# 4. Add initial branch lengths
# ----------------------------
tree$edge.length <- rep(1, nrow(tree$edge))

# ----------------------------
# 5. Time-calibrate with chronos
# ----------------------------
dl <- chronos(tree)

# ----------------------------
# 6. Ensure dl keeps the cleaned labels
# ----------------------------
dl$tip.label <- tree$tip.label

# ----------------------------
# 7. Plot the dated tree
# ----------------------------
plot(dl, cex = 0.8, font = 3)   # italic labels


```
```{r fig1a}

tiff(file.path(output_dir,"datedPrimateTree.tiff"))

print(
  plot(dl, cex = 0.8, font = 3)
)
axisPhylo()
title("Dated Primate Tree")

dev.off()

```


```{r, fig 1a}

# for mac users

# if(length(dl$phylo) == 0){
#   stop("No chronograms found for this species list. Check species names.")
# }
# 

# for windows users

if (!inherits(dl, "phylo") ||
    length(dl$tip.label) == 0 ||
    is.null(dl$edge.length)) {
  stop("No valid chronogram was produced. Check species names or tree construction.")
}

outfile <- "primate_timetree.nwk"
write.tree(dl, file = outfile)
message("Saved time-calibrated tree to: ", outfile)

tree <- dl
node_depths <- node.depth.edgelength(tree)

###Flip so tips = 0, root = max divergence
tree$edge.length <- max(node_depths) - node_depths[tree$edge[,2]]

tiff(file.path(output_dir, "PrimateTree.tiff"), width = 2000, height = 2000, res = 300)

tree <- ladderize(tree, right = FALSE)

p <- ggtree(tree) +
  geom_tiplab(size = 3) +
  theme_tree2()

print(p)

dev.off()


```








## FIGURE 1: Tree Metrics (from iqtree3)
any reference to pathway is a reference to functional category
```{r, tree metrics, COMPLETE}

set.seed(100)

Category_order <- c(
  "Taurine and cysteine metabolism",
  "Retinoid / vitamin A metabolism",
  "Glycine metabolism",
  "Fatty acid & cholesterol metabolism",
  "Bile acid biosynthesis & metabolism"
)


theme_bold <- theme(
  axis.title.x = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
  axis.title.y = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
  axis.text.x  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, color = "black"),
  axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", color = "black"),
  legend.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 8)), 
  legend.text  = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
  plot.title   = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", hjust = .5),
  axis.line    = element_line(size = 1.6, color = "black"),
  legend.position = "top",
 panel.grid = element_blank(),
 plot.margin = margin(15, 160, 15, 15),
 plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
)


use_per_site <- FALSE
min_genes <- 0
max_genes <- Inf
fdr_cutoff <- 0.10
hometree <- read_csv("iqtree_metrics.csv")
gene_metadata <- read_csv("gene_sets_union.csv")

df <- left_join(hometree, gene_metadata, by = c("gene_id" = "symbol")) %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      c(
        raw_unconstrained_lnL, raw_consensus_lnL, rf_distance_ml_vs_consensus,
        n_sequences, n_parsimony_informative_sites, prop_invariable_sites,
        raw_internal_length, n_sites, pct_constant_sites
      ),
      as.numeric
    ),
    delta_lnL = raw_unconstrained_lnL - raw_consensus_lnL,
    D = -2 * (raw_consensus_lnL - raw_unconstrained_lnL),
    D_per_pi = if_else(n_parsimony_informative_sites > 0, D / n_parsimony_informative_sites, NA_real_),
    rf_rel = if_else(2 * n_sequences - 6 > 0, rf_distance_ml_vs_consensus / (2 * n_sequences - 6), NA_real_),
    proportion_variable = 1 - prop_invariable_sites,
    effect = if (use_per_site) D_per_pi else D
  )

df <- df %>%
  rename(functional_category = pathway_label)

df <- filter_one2manyID(df, "gene_id")

df <- exorcise(df, "gene_id")

main_df <- df %>%
  filter(
    !tolower(as.character(has_high_gap_warning)) %in% c("true","t","1"),
    !is.na(functional_category ), !is.na(rf_rel), n_sequences >= 10
  ) %>%
  distinct(gene_id,functional_category, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    delta_sign = case_when(
      effect > 0 ~ "positive",
      effect < 0 ~ "negative",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(delta_sign))




gene_sets <- main_df %>%
  distinct(gene_id,functional_category) %>%
  mutate(value = 1L) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from =functional_category, values_from = value, values_fill = 0L) %>%
  as.data.frame()

pathways <- setdiff(names(gene_sets), "gene_id")
gene_sets[pathways] <- lapply(gene_sets[pathways], as.integer)


op <- par(
  cex = 2,
  font = 2,
  cex.axis = 2,
  col = "black",
  fg  = "black",
  cex.lab = 2,
  cex.main = 2,
  mar = c(2, 2, 2, 2),   # bottom, left, top, right
  oma = c(0, 0, 0, 0),
  xpd = NA,
  col.axis = "black",
  font.axis = 2
)


pA <- UpSetR::upset(
  gene_sets,
  sets = pathways,
  order.by = "freq",
  nsets = length(pathways),
  matrix.color = "black",
  main.bar.color = "grey10",
  sets.bar.color = "grey10",


  # match point + line styling
  point.size = 3,
  line.size = 1.5,

  # match text styling
  text.scale = c(2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2),
  set_size.show = FALSE
)

 par(op)




pw <- combn(pathways, 2)
table1 <- data.frame(
  pathway1 = pw[1, ],
  pathway2 = pw[2, ],
  genes = I(apply(pw, 2, function(x) gene_sets$gene_id[gene_sets[[x[1]]] == 1 & gene_sets[[x[2]]] == 1])),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  filter(lengths(genes) > 0) %>%
  mutate(n_overlap = lengths(genes))

path_df <- main_df %>%
  select(gene_id,functional_category, effect, rf_rel) %>%
  filter(!is.na(effect), !is.na(rf_rel)) %>%
  add_count(functional_category, name = "n") %>%
  filter(n >= min_genes, n <= max_genes) %>%
  select(-n)

ad_vs_other_pathways_full <- path_df %>%
  group_by(functional_category) %>%
  summarise(
    n_pathway = n(),
    n_other_pathways = sum(path_df$functional_category != first(functional_category )),
    median_effect_pathway = median(effect, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_effect_other_pathways = median(path_df$effect[path_df$functional_category  != first(functional_category)], na.rm = TRUE),
    median_rf_pathway = median(rf_rel, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_rf_other_pathways = median(path_df$rf_rel[path_df$functional_category  != first(functional_category)], na.rm = TRUE),
    p_ad_effect = if (n() >= 5 && n_other_pathways >= 5) {
      tryCatch(
        as.numeric(kSamples::ad.test(
          effect,
          path_df$effect[path_df$functional_category != first(functional_category)],
          method = "asymptotic"
        )$ad[1, " asympt. P-value"]),
        error = function(e) NA_real_
      )
    } else NA_real_,
    p_ad_rf = if (n() >= 5 && n_other_pathways >= 5) {
      tryCatch(
        as.numeric(kSamples::ad.test(
          rf_rel,
          path_df$rf_rel[path_df$functional_category != first(functional_category)],
          method = "asymptotic"
        )$ad[1, " asympt. P-value"]),
        error = function(e) NA_real_
      )
    } else NA_real_,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    fdr_ad_effect = p.adjust(p_ad_effect, method = "BH"),
    fdr_ad_rf = p.adjust(p_ad_rf, method = "BH")
  ) %>%
  arrange(fdr_ad_rf, fdr_ad_effect, p_ad_rf, p_ad_effect)

ad_vs_other_pathways_sig <- ad_vs_other_pathways_full %>%
  filter(if_any(starts_with("fdr_ad_"), ~coalesce(.x <= fdr_cutoff, FALSE)))

plot_df_B <- path_df %>%
  mutate(
    pathway_chr=as.character(functional_category),
    sig_path=pathway_chr %in% ad_vs_other_pathways_sig$functional_category 
  )
path_levels_B <- plot_df_B %>%
  group_by(pathway_chr) %>%
  summarise(median_effect=median(effect,na.rm=TRUE),.groups="drop") %>%
  arrange(median_effect) %>%
  pull(pathway_chr)
plot_df_B$pathway_plot <- factor(plot_df_B$pathway_chr, levels= Category_order)

star_df_B <- plot_df_B %>%
  group_by(pathway_plot) %>%
  summarise(
    y_star = max(effect, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.05 * diff(range(plot_df_B$effect, na.rm = TRUE)),
    sig_path = first(sig_path),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(sig_path)

rf_lab <- "Relative RF distance\n(0 = identical topology, 1 = maximally different)"
ylab_txt <- if (use_per_site) {
  "Gene-tree support relative to the consensus tree\n(D per informative site)"
} else {
  "Gene-tree support relative to the consensus tree\n(D = 2 × [lnL_gene tree − lnL_consensus tree])"
}




#title and subtitle 

 # title = "Panel B. Pathway distributions of gene-tree support values",

  # subtitle = paste0(
    #   "Each pathway was compared against all OTHER pathway-labeled genes combined using Anderson–Darling tests on effect and RF distributions.\n",
    #   "Asterisks mark pathways with BH-adjusted p-value ≤ ", fdr_cutoff, " for at least one test."
    # )

pB <- ggplot(plot_df_B, aes(pathway_plot, effect)) +
  geom_violin(aes(fill = sig_path), color = "grey40", scale = "width", alpha = 0.9, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = rf_rel), width = 0.12, size = 1.3, alpha = 0.65) +
  stat_summary(fun = median, geom = "point", color = "black", size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  geom_text(data = star_df_B, aes(pathway_plot, y_star), label = "*", inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(`FALSE` = "grey92", `TRUE` = "#f4a261"), guide = "none") +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option="C",name=rf_lab) +
  scale_x_discrete() +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +
  annotate(
    "text", x = Inf, y = Inf,
    label = if (nrow(ad_vs_other_pathways_sig)) {
      paste0(
        ad_vs_other_pathways_sig$functional_category,
        ": effect FDR=", signif(ad_vs_other_pathways_sig$fdr_ad_effect, 2),
        ", RF FDR=", signif(ad_vs_other_pathways_sig$fdr_ad_rf, 2),
        collapse = "\n"
      )
    } else {
      paste0("No pathways with BH-adjusted AD p-value ≤ ", fdr_cutoff, " for effect or RF")
    },
    hjust = .92, vjust = 1.05, size = 3, lineheight = 1
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Functional Category",
    y = ylab_txt,
    # title = "Panel B. Pathway distributions of gene-tree support values",
    # subtitle = paste0(
    #   "Each pathway was compared against all OTHER pathway-labeled genes combined using Anderson–Darling tests on effect and RF distributions.\n",
    #   "Asterisks mark pathways with BH-adjusted p-value ≤ ", fdr_cutoff, " for at least one test."
    # )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 220, 5.5, 5.5)
  )

pB

pB <- pB +
  # theme + spacing
  theme_bold +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15))) +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +
  theme(legend.position = "none") 





### --- Save flipped Panel B ---
ggsave(file.path(output_dir,"pBViolin.tiff"), pB, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300, bg = "white")




path_stats <- path_df %>%
  group_by( functional_category ) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median_effect = median(effect, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_rf = median(rf_rel, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

pw_pairs <- t(combn(sort(unique(as.character(path_df$functional_category))), 2)) %>%
  as.data.frame(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
names(pw_pairs) <- c("category_1","category_2")

ad_pairwise_pathways_full <- pw_pairs %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    n_1 = path_stats$n[match(category_1, path_stats$functional_category)],
    n_2 = path_stats$n[match(category_2, path_stats$functional_category)],
    median_effect_1 = path_stats$median_effect[match(category_1, path_stats$functional_category)],
    median_effect_2 = path_stats$median_effect[match(category_2, path_stats$functional_category)],
    median_rf_1 = path_stats$median_rf[match(category_1, path_stats$functional_category)],
    median_rf_2 = path_stats$median_rf[match(category_2, path_stats$functional_category)],
    p_ad_effect = {
      x <- path_df$effect[path_df$functional_category == category_1]
      y <- path_df$effect[path_df$functional_category == category_2]
      if (length(x) >= 5 && length(y) >= 5) {
        tryCatch(as.numeric(kSamples::ad.test(x, y, method = "asymptotic")$ad[1, " asympt. P-value"]),
                 error = function(e) NA_real_)
      } else NA_real_
    },
    p_ad_rf = {
      x <- path_df$rf_rel[path_df$functional_category == category_1]
      y <- path_df$rf_rel[path_df$functional_category == category_2]
      if (length(x) >= 5 && length(y) >= 5) {
        tryCatch(as.numeric(kSamples::ad.test(x, y, method = "asymptotic")$ad[1, " asympt. P-value"]),
                 error = function(e) NA_real_)
      } else NA_real_
    }
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    fdr_ad_effect = p.adjust(p_ad_effect, method = "BH"),
    fdr_ad_rf = p.adjust(p_ad_rf, method = "BH")
  ) %>%
  arrange(fdr_ad_rf, fdr_ad_effect, p_ad_rf, p_ad_effect)

ad_pairwise_pathways_sig <- ad_pairwise_pathways_full %>%
  filter(if_any(starts_with("fdr_ad_"), ~coalesce(.x <= fdr_cutoff, FALSE)))

plot_df_C <- path_df %>%
  mutate(
    pathway_chr=as.character( functional_category ),
    sig_path=pathway_chr %in% c(ad_pairwise_pathways_sig$category_1,ad_pairwise_pathways_sig$category_2)
  )
path_levels_C <- plot_df_C %>%
  group_by(pathway_chr) %>%
  summarise(median_effect=median(effect,na.rm=TRUE),.groups="drop") %>%
  arrange(median_effect) %>%
  pull(pathway_chr)
plot_df_C$pathway_plot <- factor(plot_df_C$pathway_chr, levels=Category_order)

pair_brackets_C <- ad_pairwise_pathways_sig %>%
  mutate(
    x1 = match(category_1, levels(plot_df_C$pathway_plot)),
    x2 = match(category_2, levels(plot_df_C$pathway_plot)),
    y = max(plot_df_C$effect, na.rm = TRUE) +
      row_number() * 0.06 * diff(range(plot_df_C$effect, na.rm = TRUE)),
    x_mid = (x1 + x2) / 2
  )


#title and subtitle 

    # title = "Panel C. Pathway distributions of gene-tree support values",
    # subtitle = paste0(
    #   "Pathways were compared pairwise using Anderson–Darling tests on effect and RF distributions.\n",
    #   "Brackets mark pathway pairs with BH-adjusted p-value ≤ ", fdr_cutoff, "."
    # )

pC <- ggplot(plot_df_C, aes(pathway_plot, effect)) +
  geom_violin(aes(fill = sig_path), color = "grey40", scale = "width", alpha = 0.9, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = rf_rel), width = 0.12, size = 1.3, alpha = 0.65) +
  stat_summary(fun = median, geom = "point", color = "black", size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  geom_segment(data = pair_brackets_C, aes(x = x1, xend = x2, y = y, yend = y), inherit.aes = FALSE, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_segment(data = pair_brackets_C, aes(x = x1, xend = x1, y = y, yend = y - 0.015 * diff(range(plot_df_C$effect, na.rm = TRUE))), inherit.aes = FALSE, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_segment(data = pair_brackets_C, aes(x = x2, xend = x2, y = y, yend = y - 0.015 * diff(range(plot_df_C$effect, na.rm = TRUE))), inherit.aes = FALSE, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(data = pair_brackets_C, aes(x = x_mid, y = y + 0.015 * diff(range(plot_df_C$effect, na.rm = TRUE))), label = "*", inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(`FALSE` = "grey92", `TRUE` = "#f4a261"), guide = "none") +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "C", name = "Relative RF distance\n(0 = identical topology, 1 = maximally different)") +
  scale_x_discrete() +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +
  annotate(
    "text", x = Inf, y = Inf,
    label = if (nrow(ad_pairwise_pathways_sig)) {
      paste0(
        ad_pairwise_pathways_sig$category_1, " vs ", ad_pairwise_pathways_sig$category_2,
        ": effect FDR=", signif(ad_pairwise_pathways_sig$fdr_ad_effect, 2),
        ", RF FDR=", signif(ad_pairwise_pathways_sig$fdr_ad_rf, 2),
        collapse = "\n"
      )
    } else {
      paste0("No pathway pairs with BH-adjusted AD p-value ≤ ", fdr_cutoff, " for effect or RF")
    },
    hjust = .957, vjust = 1.05, size = 3, lineheight = 1
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Functional Category",
    y = ylab_txt,
    # title = "Panel C. Pathway distributions of gene-tree support values",
    # subtitle = paste0(
    #   "Pathways were compared pairwise using Anderson–Darling tests on effect and RF distributions.\n",
    #   "Brackets mark pathway pairs with BH-adjusted p-value ≤ ", fdr_cutoff, "."
    # )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 220, 5.5, 5.5)
  )



# Compute max rf_rel per pathway
max_rf <- plot_df_C %>%
  group_by(pathway_plot) %>%
  summarise(max_rf = max(rf_rel, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(x_pos = as.numeric(factor(pathway_plot)))


# Join into bracket table
pair_brackets_C <- pair_brackets_C %>%
  left_join(max_rf, by = c("x1" = "x_pos")) %>%
  rename(max_rf_x1 = max_rf) %>%
  left_join(max_rf, by = c("x2" = "x_pos")) %>%
  rename(max_rf_x2 = max_rf)


# Set bracket height slightly above the max
pair_brackets_C$y_rf <- pmax(pair_brackets_C$max_rf_x1,
                             pair_brackets_C$max_rf_x2) +
  0.03 * diff(range(plot_df_C$rf_rel, na.rm = TRUE))


pCf <- ggplot(plot_df_C, aes(pathway_plot, rf_rel)) +
  geom_violin(aes(fill = sig_path), color = "grey40", scale = "width",
              alpha = 0.9, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = effect), width = 0.12, size = 1.3, alpha = 0.65) +
  stat_summary(fun = median, geom = "point", color = "black", size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +

  # --- bracket segments now use rf_rel range instead of effect ---
  geom_segment(
    data = pair_brackets_C,
    aes(x = x1, xend = x2, y = y_rf, yend = y_rf),
    inherit.aes = FALSE, linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = pair_brackets_C,
    aes(x = x1, xend = x1,
        y = y_rf,
        yend = y_rf - 0.015 * diff(range(plot_df_C$rf_rel, na.rm = TRUE))),
    inherit.aes = FALSE, linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = pair_brackets_C,
    aes(x = x2, xend = x2,
        y = y_rf,
        yend = y_rf - 0.015 * diff(range(plot_df_C$rf_rel, na.rm = TRUE))),
    inherit.aes = FALSE, linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = pair_brackets_C,
    aes(
      x = x_mid,
      y = y_rf + 0.015 * diff(range(plot_df_C$rf_rel, na.rm = TRUE))
    ),
    label = "*", inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 5, fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(values = c(`FALSE` = "grey92", `TRUE` = "#f4a261"), guide = "none") +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "C", name = "Effect size") +
  scale_x_discrete() +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +

  annotate(
    "text", x = Inf, y = Inf,
    label = if (nrow(ad_pairwise_pathways_sig)) {
      paste0(
        ad_pairwise_pathways_sig$category_1, " vs ", ad_pairwise_pathways_sig$category_2,
        ": effect FDR=", signif(ad_pairwise_pathways_sig$fdr_ad_effect, 2),
        ", RF FDR=", signif(ad_pairwise_pathways_sig$fdr_ad_rf, 2),
        collapse = "\n"
      )
    } else {
      paste0("No pathway pairs with BH-adjusted AD p-value ≤ ", fdr_cutoff, " for effect or RF")
    },
   hjust = .735, vjust = 0.5, size = 3.8, lineheight = 1,  color = "grey15",  
  fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Functional Category",
    y = "Relative RF distance\n(0 = identical topology, 1 = maximally different)"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 220, 5.5, 5.5)
  )


pCf <- pCf +
  # theme + spacing
  theme_bold +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15))) +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")



### --- Save flipped Panel C ---
ggsave(file.path(output_dir,"pCViolinFlipped.tiff"), pCf, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

#Legends

# Temporarily add legend back with  styling
pB_with_legend <- pB +
  guides(
    color = guide_colorbar(
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      barwidth = unit(10, "cm"),
      barheight = unit(0.4, "cm")
    )
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")
  )

# Extract legend
legend_B <- get_legend(pB_with_legend)
legend_B_plot <- ggdraw(legend_B)

# Save legend
ggsave(file.path(output_dir,
  "legend_panelB.tiff"),
  legend_B_plot,
  width = 6,
  height = 2,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

pC_with_legend <- pC +
  guides(
    color = guide_colorbar(
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      barwidth = unit(10, "cm"),
      barheight = unit(0.4, "cm")
    )
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")
  )

legend_C <- get_legend(pC_with_legend)
legend_C_plot <- ggdraw(legend_C)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir,
  "legend_panelC.tiff"),
  legend_C_plot,
  width = 6,
  height = 2,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


legend_title <- "Gene-tree support relative to the consensus tree\n (D = 2 × [lnL_gene tree – lnL_consensus tree])"

pCf_with_legend <- pCf +
  scale_color_viridis_c(
    option = "C",        # ← KEEP THIS or colors will change
    name = legend_title
  ) +
  guides(
    color = guide_colorbar(
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      barwidth = unit(10, "cm"),
      barheight = unit(0.4, "cm"),
      title.theme = element_text(
        size = 10,
        face = "bold",
        hjust = 0.5
      )
    )
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold")
  )

legend_C <- get_legend(pCf_with_legend)
legend_C_plot <- ggdraw(legend_C)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir,
  "legend_panelCflipped.tiff"),
  legend_C_plot,
  width = 6,
  height = 2,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)

# Compute max rf_rel per pathway for star placement
max_rf_B <- plot_df_B %>%
  group_by(pathway_plot) %>%
  summarise(max_rf = max(rf_rel, na.rm = TRUE))

# Join into star_df_B
star_df_B <- star_df_B %>%
  left_join(max_rf_B, by = "pathway_plot")

# New star height in rf_rel space
star_df_B$y_star_rf <- star_df_B$max_rf +
  0.03 * diff(range(plot_df_B$rf_rel, na.rm = TRUE))



### --- Build flipped Panel B (pBf) ---
pBf <- ggplot(plot_df_B, aes(pathway_plot, rf_rel)) +
  geom_violin(aes(fill = sig_path), color = "grey40", scale = "width",
              alpha = 0.9, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = effect), width = 0.12, size = 1.3, alpha = 0.65) +
  stat_summary(fun = median, geom = "point", color = "black", size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +

  # --- stars now use y_star_rf ---
  geom_text(
    data = star_df_B,
    aes(x = pathway_plot, y = y_star_rf),
    label = "*",
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(values = c(`FALSE` = "grey92", `TRUE` = "#f4a261"), guide = "none") +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "C", name = "Effect size") +
  scale_x_discrete() +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +

  annotate(
    "text", x = Inf, y = Inf,
    label = if (nrow(ad_vs_other_pathways_sig)) {
      paste0(
        ad_vs_other_pathways_sig$functional_category,
        ": effect FDR=", signif(ad_vs_other_pathways_sig$fdr_ad_effect, 2),
        ", RF FDR=", signif(ad_vs_other_pathways_sig$fdr_ad_rf, 2),
        collapse = "\n"
      )
    } else {
      paste0("No pathways with BH-adjusted AD p-value ≤ ", fdr_cutoff, " for effect or RF")
    },
    hjust = 1, vjust = 0.3, size = 3.9, lineheight = 1, color = "grey15",  
  fontface = "bold"
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Functional Category",
    y = "Relative RF distance\n(0 = identical topology, 1 = maximally different)"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(5.5, 220, 5.5, 5.5)
  ) +

  # Final formatting
  theme_bold +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15))) +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")



### --- Save flipped Panel B ---
ggsave(file.path(output_dir,"pBViolinFlipped.tiff"), pBf, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300, bg = "white")


legend_title <- "Gene-tree support relative to the consensus tree\n (D = 2 × [lnL_gene tree – lnL_consensus tree])"

pBf_with_legend <- pBf +
  scale_color_viridis_c(
    option = "C",        # ← KEEP THIS or colors will change
    name = legend_title
  ) +
  guides(
    color = guide_colorbar(
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      barwidth = unit(10, "cm"),
      barheight = unit(0.4, "cm"),
      title.theme = element_text(
        size = 10,
        face = "bold",
        hjust = 0.5
      )
    )
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold")
  )

legend_Bf <- get_legend(pBf_with_legend)
legend_Bf_plot <- ggdraw(legend_Bf)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir,
  "legend_panelBflipped.tiff"),
  legend_Bf_plot,
  width = 6,
  height = 2,
  dpi = 300,
  bg = "white"
)


ad_vs_other_pathways_sig
ad_pairwise_pathways_sig
table1



tiff(file.path(output_dir,"pA_UpSet.tiff"), width = 12, height = 6, units = "in", res = 300)


pA 


dev.off()


pA
pB
pC

table1 <- table1 %>%
  rename(
    category1 = pathway1,
    category2 = pathway2
  )

ad_pairwise_categories_sig <- ad_pairwise_pathways_sig %>%
  rename(
    category1 = category_1,
    category2 = category_2
  )

ad_vs_other_categories_sig <- ad_vs_other_pathways_sig %>%
  rename(
    n_category = n_pathway
  )



write_tsv(
  table1,
  file = file.path(output_dir, "table1.tsv")
)
write.csv(ad_pairwise_categories_sig,
          file.path(output_dir, "ad_pairwise_categories.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(ad_vs_other_categories_sig,
          file.path(output_dir, "ad_vs_other_categories_sig"),
          row.names = FALSE)

```






## FIGURE 2: HyPhy: GARD (Recombination/Breakpoint support analysis)
NOTE: GARD DOES NOT OFFER BRANCH level (therefore per primate species/sequence-lvl information).
It makes inferences about global breakpoints/recombination sites based on modeling global alignment pattern

```{r, GARD}

theme_bold_breakpoints <- theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

# GARD 3-panel figure: breakpoints, breakpoint-count distribution, pathway enrichment
min_nseq <- 8
top_n_genes <- 5            # top N per pathway to show in Panel A

#####readd input files (adapt filenames if needed) ----------------
gard_res <- read.csv("tgard_summary.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

gard_res <- filter_one2manyID(gard_res, "gene")

gard_res <- exorcise(gard_res, "gene")

function_metadata <- read_csv("gene_sets_union.csv")
gard_res <- merge(gard_res, function_metadata, by.x = "gene", by.y = "symbol", all.x = TRUE)
gene_length <- read_tsv("gene_lengths.tsv")
gene_length <- gene_length %>% 
  group_by(gene_dir) %>% 
  mutate(avg_length = mean(seq_length, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  dplyr::select(c(gene_dir, avg_length)) %>% ungroup()

# gard_res <- merge(gard_res, gene_length, by.x = "gene", by.y = "gene_dir") %>% unique() %>%
#   mutate(n_seq_gard = map_int(sequence_names, ~ sum(nzchar(str_trim(
#     str_split(str_replace_all(str_remove_all(as.character(.x), "^\\s*\\[|\\]\\s*$"), "[\"'\\\\]", ""), "\\s*,\\s*")[[1]]
#   ))))) %>%
#   filter(n_seq_gard >= 5)


gard_res <- merge(gard_res, gene_length, by.x = "gene", by.y = "gene_dir") %>% unique() %>%
  filter(is.na(number_of_sequences) | number_of_sequences >= 10)

#######Parsing helpers (robust) ----------------
parse_numvec <- function(s) {
  if (is.null(s) || is.na(s) || !nzchar(as.character(s))) return(integer(0))
  if (is.numeric(s)) return(as.numeric(s))
  s_chr <- as.character(s)
  out <- tryCatch(fromJSON(s_chr), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (!is.null(out)) {
    vals <- unlist(out)
    return(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(vals)))
  }
  s2 <- str_remove_all(s_chr, "^\\s*\\[|\\]\\s*$")
  s2 <- str_replace_all(s2, "[\\[\\]]", "")
  toks <- str_split(s2, ",")[[1]] %>% str_trim()
  nums <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(toks))
  nums[!is.na(nums)]
}

parse_partitions <- function(s) {
  if (is.null(s) || is.na(s) || !nzchar(as.character(s))) return(NULL)
  s_chr <- as.character(s)
  out <- tryCatch(fromJSON(s_chr), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (!is.null(out)) return(out)
  m <- str_match_all(s_chr, "\\[\\s*(\\d+)\\s*,\\s*(\\d+)\\s*\\]")[[1]]
  if (nrow(m) == 0) return(NULL)
  lapply(seq_len(nrow(m)), function(i) c(as.integer(m[i,2]), as.integer(m[i,3])))
}

####Build partition aggregates ----------------
partition_agg <- gard_res %>%
  filter(!is.na(partition_nt_range) & nzchar(as.character(partition_nt_range))) %>%
  mutate(
    .part  = suppressWarnings(as.integer(partition)),
    .start = suppressWarnings(as.integer(str_trim(sub("^\\s*(\\d+)\\s*-.*$", "\\1", as.character(partition_nt_range))))),
    .end   = suppressWarnings(as.integer(str_trim(sub("^.*-(\\s*\\d+)\\s*$", "\\1", as.character(partition_nt_range)))))
  ) %>%
  distinct(gene, .part, .start, .end) %>%
  arrange(gene, .part) %>%
  group_by(gene) %>%
  summarize(
    final_breaks = list(if (n() <= 1) integer(0) else head(.end[order(.part)], -1)),
    n_partitions = n(),
    n_final_breaks = ifelse(n_partitions <= 1, 0L, n_partitions - 1L),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

gard_res <- gard_res %>%
  rename(functional_category = pathway_label)

#####Build compact summary table
gard_compact <- gard_res %>%
  mutate(
    number_of_sequences_numeric = suppressWarnings(as.integer(number_of_sequences)),
    aln_len = suppressWarnings(as.integer(number_of_sites))
  ) %>%
  filter(is.na(number_of_sequences_numeric) | number_of_sequences_numeric >= min_nseq) %>%
  mutate(
    sum_deltaAICc = map_dbl(improvements_deltaAICc, ~ sum(parse_numvec(.x), na.rm = TRUE)),
    max_deltaAICc = map_dbl(improvements_deltaAICc, ~ { v <- parse_numvec(.x); if (length(v)>0) max(v, na.rm=TRUE) else NA_real_ }),
    partition_count_parsed = map_int(partition_bps, ~ { parts <- parse_partitions(.x); if (is.null(parts)) 0L else length(parts) }),
    site_positions_parsed = map(site_positions, parse_numvec),
    site_supports_parsed  = map(site_supports, parse_numvec)
  ) %>%
  mutate(avg_length = ifelse(is.na(as.numeric(avg_length)), suppressWarnings(as.numeric(number_of_sites)), as.numeric(avg_length))) %>%
  distinct(gene,functional_category, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  left_join(partition_agg, by = "gene") %>%
  mutate(
    final_breaks = ifelse(map_lgl(final_breaks, is.null), list(integer(0)), final_breaks),
    n_final_breaks = coalesce(n_final_breaks, 0L),
    n_partitions = coalesce(n_partitions, 1L)
  ) %>%
  mutate(median_deltaAICc = map_dbl(improvements_deltaAICc, ~ { v <- parse_numvec(.x); if(length(v)==0) NA_real_ else median(v, na.rm=TRUE) }))

#Panel A: Histogram of breakpoint counts across genes ----------------
# compute count of final breaks per gene (use gard_compact authoritative)
break_counts <- gard_compact %>%
  transmute(gene,functional_category, n_final_breaks = as.integer(n_final_breaks)) %>%
  mutate(n_final_breaks = ifelse(is.na(n_final_breaks), 0L, n_final_breaks))

###histo barplot
break_counts_summary <- break_counts %>%
  group_by(n_final_breaks) %>%
  summarise(n_genes = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(n_final_breaks)




#title 

     # title = "Panel A — Distribution of final breakpoint counts across genes"

pA <- ggplot(break_counts_summary, aes(x = as.factor(n_final_breaks), y = n_genes)) +
  geom_col(fill = "firebrick", alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n_genes), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  coord_flip(clip = "off") +
  labs(x = "Number of GARD final breakpoints (per gene)", y = "Number of genes") +
       # title = "Panel A — Distribution of final breakpoint counts across genes")
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank()
        )



pA

pA <- pA + theme_bold_breakpoints 

pA

ggsave(file.path(output_dir,"PanelA_breakpoint_counts.tiff"),pA,
  width = 7.5,
  height = 4.2,
  dpi = 300,
  units = "in",
  bg = "white",
  compression = "lzw"
)

################DATA PREP FOR ADV OF PANEL B########################
top_by_pathway <- gard_compact %>%
  filter(!is.na( functional_category )) %>%
  arrange( functional_category , desc(n_final_breaks), desc(median_deltaAICc)) %>%  # use median now
  group_by( functional_category ) %>%
  slice_head(n = top_n_genes) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    gene_label = paste0(gene, " (n=", n_final_breaks, ")"),
    gene_label = ifelse(is.na(gene_label), gene, gene_label)
  ) %>%
  group_by( functional_category ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_final_breaks), desc(median_deltaAICc)) %>%
  mutate(gene_ord = factor(gene_label, levels = rev(unique(gene_label)))) %>%
  ungroup()

bp_long <- top_by_pathway %>%
  filter(!is.na(avg_length)) %>%
  mutate(final_breaks = map(final_breaks, ~ as.integer(.x[!is.na(.x)]))) %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, gene_label,functional_category, avg_length, final_breaks) %>%
  unnest_longer(final_breaks, values_to = "breakpoint") %>%
  mutate(breakpoint = as.integer(breakpoint)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(breakpoint) & breakpoint >= 1 & breakpoint <= avg_length)

lm_df <- top_by_pathway %>%
  filter(!is.na(avg_length)) %>%
  dplyr::select(gene, gene_label,functional_category, avg_length, n_final_breaks) %>%
  mutate(avg_length = as.numeric(avg_length),
         n_final_breaks = as.numeric(n_final_breaks))

#Ffitinbgt linear model per path
lm_results <- lm_df %>%
  group_by( functional_category ) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    df <- .x
    if(nrow(df) < 2) {
      tibble(
        slope = NA_real_,
        intercept = NA_real_,
        r2 = NA_real_,
        p_value = NA_real_,
        label = "n<2"
      )
    } else {
      fit <- lm(n_final_breaks ~ avg_length, data = df)
      s <- summary(fit)
      slope <- coef(fit)["avg_length"]
      intercept <- coef(fit)["(Intercept)"]
      r2 <- s$r.squared
      p_value <- s$coefficients["avg_length", "Pr(>|t|)"]  # <- now correct
      tibble(
        slope = slope,
        intercept = intercept,
        r2 = r2,
        p_value = p_value,
        label = sprintf("R²=%.2f\np=%.3g", r2, p_value)
      )
    }
  }, .keep = TRUE) %>%
  ungroup()



#deinfen bars top df pathwayts
bars_df <- top_by_pathway %>%
  filter(!is.na(avg_length)) %>%
  left_join(lm_results %>% dplyr::select(functional_category , label), by = "functional_category")

# 4) Plot with slope annotations
# pB <- ggplot() +
#   geom_segment(data = bars_df, aes(x = 1, xend = avg_length, y = gene_label, yend = gene_label),
#                colour = "grey85", size = 6, lineend = "butt") +
#   geom_point(data = bp_long, aes(x = breakpoint, y = gene_label), shape = "|", size = 4, color = "firebrick") +
#   geom_text(
#   data = lm_results,
#   aes(x = Inf, y = Inf, label = label),
#   inherit.aes = FALSE,
#   hjust = 1.2,   # pulls text into the margin
#   vjust = 1.1,
#   size = 3
# ) +
#    coord_flip(clip = "off") +
#   facet_wrap(~functional_category, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
#   scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.01))) +
#   scale_y_discrete(guide = guide_axis(n.dodge = 3)) +
#   labs(x = "Position along gene (codons)", y = NULL,
#        title = "Panel B — Breakpoint positions (top genes per pathway)") +
#  theme(legend.position = "none", panel.grid = element_blank(),plot.margin = margin(10, 40, 10, 10) 
#         )

#title
     # title = "Panel B — Breakpoint positions (top genes per pathway)"

bars_df$functional_category <- factor(bars_df$functional_category, levels = Category_order)

pB <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(data = bars_df, aes(x = 1, xend = avg_length, y = gene_label, yend = gene_label),
               colour = "grey85", size = 6, lineend = "butt") +
  geom_point(data = bp_long, aes(x = breakpoint, y = gene_label), shape = "|", size = 4, color = "firebrick") +
  geom_text(
  data = lm_results,
  aes(x = Inf, y = Inf, label = label),
  inherit.aes = FALSE,
  hjust = 1.2,   # pulls text into the margin
  vjust = 2.1,
  size = 4
) +
  facet_wrap(~functional_category, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.01))) +
  labs(x = "Position along gene (codons)", y = NULL,
       # title = "Panel B — Breakpoint positions (top genes per pathway)"
       ) +
 theme(legend.position = "none", panel.grid = element_blank(),plot.margin = margin(10, 40, 10, 10) 
        )

pB

pB <- pB + theme_bold_breakpoints + theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),plot.margin = margin(10, 40, 10, 10)) 

pB

ggsave(file.path(output_dir,"PanelB_pathway_violin.tiff"),pB,
  width = 11,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300,
  units = "in",
  bg = "white",
  compression = "lzw"
)


pB <- pB + theme_bold_breakpoints + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold", color = "black"), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold", color = "black"),panel.grid = element_blank(),plot.margin = margin(10, 40, 10, 10)) 

pA <- pA + theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold", color = "black"))

# Combine with existing panels if present
fig_left  <- (pA / pB)
print(fig_left)


ggsave(file.path(output_dir,
  filename = "Figure2.tiff"),
  plot = fig_left,
  width = 14,
  height = 8.5,
  dpi = 300,
  units = "in",
  bg = "white",
  compression = "lzw"
)



```



```{r}

# Panel C: Pathway over-representation of recombination-positive genes (length-controlled)
# parameters
min_genes_in_pathway <- 0
n_perm <- 10000
set.seed(2026)

# --- Build breakpoint-long table and length-adjusted residuals ---
bp_long <- gard_compact %>%
  distinct(gene, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  filter(!is.na( functional_category ) & !is.na(avg_length)) %>%
  mutate(
    avg_length = as.numeric(avg_length),
    final_breaks = map(final_breaks, ~ as.integer(.x[!is.na(.x)]))
  ) %>%
  filter(lengths(final_breaks) > 0) %>%
  dplyr::select(gene,functional_category, avg_length, final_breaks) %>%
  unnest_longer(final_breaks, values_to = "breakpoint") %>%
  mutate(breakpoint = as.integer(breakpoint),
         rel_pos = breakpoint / avg_length) %>%
  dplyr::filter(rel_pos >= 0 & rel_pos <= 1)

# adjust relative position for gene length (keeps pairwise wilcox length-controlled)
lm_res <- lm(rel_pos ~ avg_length, data = bp_long)

##### tring to scale residuals for plotting so more intuitive
bp_long <- bp_long %>% mutate(resid_rel = resid(lm_res)) %>%
  mutate(resid_rel_scaled = (resid_rel - min(bp_long$resid_rel, na.rm = TRUE)) / (max(bp_long$resid_rel, na.rm = TRUE) - min(bp_long$resid_rel, na.rm = TRUE)))

gene_summary <- bp_long %>%
  group_by(gene,functional_category) %>%
  summarise(mean_resid_rel = mean(resid_rel, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_length = first(avg_length), .groups = "drop") %>% mutate(mean_resid_scaled = (mean_resid_rel - min(bp_long$resid_rel, na.rm = TRUE)) / (max(bp_long$resid_rel, na.rm = TRUE) - min(bp_long$resid_rel, na.rm = TRUE)))

# --- Gene-level breakpoint counts and binary predictor (include avg_length) ---
gene_break_summary <- bp_long %>%
  group_by(gene,functional_category, avg_length) %>%
  summarise(n_breaks = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(high_breaks = as.integer(n_breaks > 3),
         log_avg_length = log(avg_length),
         extreme = as.integer(n_breaks > quantile(n_breaks, 0.95)))

# ensure no NA pathway labels
gene_break_summary <- gene_break_summary %>% filter(!is.na( functional_category ))

# --- Global adjusted test: logistic model controlling for gene length ---
# tests whetherfunctional_category predicts high_breaks after adjusting for length
global_adj_fit <- glm(high_breaks ~functional_category + log_avg_length,
                      data = gene_break_summary,
                      family = binomial)

# You can inspect summary(global_adj_fit) to see which pathways differ while controlling for length.
# We'll also produce a likelihood-ratio style ANOVA for overall pathway effect:
#Table 2, not in paper as of now
global_category_anova <- anova(global_adj_fit, test = "Chisq")  # table includingfunctional_category

# --- Per-pathway adjusted logistic regressions (in_pathway vs rest), controlling for length ---
pathway_levels <- unique(gene_break_summary$functional_category )

logit_results <- map_df(pathway_levels, function(pw) {
  tmp <- gene_break_summary %>% mutate(in_pathway = as.integer( functional_category  == pw))
  fit <- glm(high_breaks ~ in_pathway + log_avg_length, data = tmp, family = binomial)
  se <- sqrt(diag(vcov(fit)))["in_pathway"]
  beta <- coef(fit)["in_pathway"]
  or <- exp(beta)
  lower <- exp(beta - 1.96 * se)
  upper <- exp(beta + 1.96 * se)
  p_raw <- summary(fit)$coefficients["in_pathway", "Pr(>|z|)"]
  tibble(pathway = pw, beta = beta, OR = or, OR_low = lower, OR_high = upper, p_raw = p_raw)
}) %>%
  mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(p_raw, method = "BH")) %>%
  arrange(p_adj)

# table of significant pathways (length-adjusted)
sig_logit_table <- logit_results %>%
  filter(p_adj < 0.1) %>%
  mutate(across(c(OR, OR_low, OR_high, p_raw, p_adj), ~ signif(.x, 3)))

# --- Visualization: ridge (original) and new proportion + forest plots (length-adjusted results) 

ridge_inset <- ggplot(bp_long, aes(x = resid_rel_scaled, y =functional_category, fill =functional_category)) +
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.7, scale = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank()) +
  labs(x = "Length-adjusted relative breakpoint (0-1 scaled)")

prop_plot <- gene_break_summary %>%
  group_by( functional_category ) %>%
  summarise(prop_high = mean(high_breaks), n_genes = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(prop_high) %>%
  mutate( functional_category  = factor( functional_category , levels =Category_order)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x =functional_category, y = prop_high, fill =functional_category)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0("n=", n_genes)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Pathway", y = "Proportion of genes with >3 breakpoints",
       title = "High-breakpoint gene proportion per pathway (unadjusted proportion)") +
  theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "none")



theme_bold <- theme(
  axis.title.x = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
  axis.title.y = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
  axis.text.x  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, color = "black"),
  axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", color = "black"),
  legend.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 8)), 
  legend.text  = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
  plot.title   = element_text(size = 16.5, face = "bold", hjust = .5),
  axis.line    = element_line(size = 1.8, color = "black"),
  legend.position = "none",
 panel.grid = element_blank()
)



##Title

 #title = "Per-pathway length-adjusted odds of having >3 breakpoints")

forest_plot <- logit_results %>%
  mutate(sig = p_adj < 0.1,
         pathway = factor(pathway, levels = Category_order)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = pathway, x = OR)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = sig), size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = OR_low, xmax = OR_high, color = sig), height = 0.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(trans = "log10") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("grey50", "red")) +
  labs(x = "Odds Ratio (High breaks >3, length-adjusted, log scale)", y = NULL,
       #  title = "Per-pathway length-adjusted odds of having >3 breakpoints"
       ) +
  theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "none") + theme_bold

ggsave(file.path(output_dir,"Forest_plotFig2.C.Tiff"),forest_plot, width = 12, height = 8)

left_panel <- ggdraw() +
  draw_plot(ridge_inset, x = 0.63, y = 0.55, width = 0.35, height = 0.35) 

right_panel <- (prop_plot / forest_plot) + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1.2))

pC_inset <- left_panel + right_panel + plot_layout(widths = c(1.1, 1))

###computing observed slopes per pathway (log-log lm)
slopes_obs <- gene_break_summary %>%
  mutate(log_n = log(n_breaks + 1), log_len = log(avg_length)) %>%
  group_by( functional_category ) %>%
  summarise(slope = coef(lm(log_n ~ log_len, data = cur_data_all()))[2],
            n = n(), .groups = "drop")
# pathway list we tested (all pathways with enough genes)
tested_paths <- slopes_obs %>% filter(n >= 5) %>% pull( functional_category )

##### one v all linear model slope of paths breakpoints
obs_diff_df <- tibble(pathway = tested_paths) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    slope_path = slopes_obs$slope[slopes_obs$functional_category == pathway],
    slope_rest = {
      rest <- filter(gene_break_summary,functional_category != pathway)
      coef(lm(log(n_breaks + 1) ~ log(avg_length), data = rest))[2]
    },
    obs_diff = slope_path - slope_rest
  ) %>% ungroup()

##### permutation test for slope-lvl differences in breakpoint distro
nperm <- 10000
perm_diffs_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = length(tested_paths), ncol = nperm,
                         dimnames = list(tested_paths, NULL))

for (i in seq_len(nperm)) {
  tmp <- gene_break_summary
  tmp$pathway_perm <- sample(tmp$functional_category)  # permute labels
  # compute slope for each pathway under permutation
  perm_slopes <- tmp %>%
    mutate(log_n = log(n_breaks + 1), log_len = log(avg_length)) %>%
    group_by(pathway_perm) %>%
    summarise(slope = if(n() >= 3) coef(lm(log_n ~ log_len, data = cur_data_all()))[2] else NA_real_,
              .groups = "drop")
  # compute slope_rest for each tested pathway and store slope_path - slope_rest
  for (p in tested_paths) {
    s_p <- perm_slopes$slope[perm_slopes$pathway_perm == p]
    # slope_rest: slope on all genes not labeled p under permutation
    rest_perm <- filter(tmp, pathway_perm != p)
    s_r <- if(nrow(rest_perm) >= 3) coef(lm(log(n_breaks + 1) ~ log(avg_length), data = rest_perm))[2] else NA_real_
    perm_diffs_mat[p, i] <- s_p - s_r
  }
}

####two side pval test pon path
emp_p <- sapply(tested_paths, function(p) {
  perm_vec <- perm_diffs_mat[p, ]
  obs_val <- obs_diff_df$obs_diff[obs_diff_df$pathway == p]
  (sum(abs(perm_vec) >= abs(obs_val), na.rm = TRUE) + 1) / (nperm + 1)
})

######Westfall-Young max-statistic FWER p-values (strong control) ----
####Build the permutation distribution of the maximum abs(perm_diff) across pathways
perm_max_abs <- apply(abs(perm_diffs_mat), 2, function(x) max(x, na.rm = TRUE))
fy_p <- sapply(tested_paths, function(p) {
  obs_val <- abs(obs_diff_df$obs_diff[obs_diff_df$pathway == p])
  (sum(perm_max_abs >= obs_val) + 1) / (nperm + 1)
})
emp_p_df <- tibble(pathway = tested_paths, p_emp = emp_p) %>%
  mutate(p_emp_BH = p.adjust(p_emp, method = "BH")) %>% mutate(p_fwer_perm = fy_p)

# Log–log scatter with fitted lines; highlight tested pathway (plab)
newdat <- expand.grid(
  avg_length = seq(min(gene_break_summary$avg_length), max(gene_break_summary$avg_length), length.out = 200),
 functional_category = unique(gene_break_summary$functional_category)
)
fit_int <- lm(log(n_breaks + 1) ~ log(avg_length) *functional_category, data = gene_break_summary)
newdat$pred <- predict(fit_int, newdat)

plab <- "Retinoid / vitamin A metabolism"
p_slope <- ggplot(gene_break_summary, aes(x = avg_length, y = n_breaks, color =functional_category)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +
  geom_line(data = newdat, aes(x = avg_length, y = exp(pred)-1, color =functional_category), size = 0.9, alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_x_log10() + scale_y_log10() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "avg_length (log)", y = "n_breaks (log)", title = paste0("Log–log slopes; highlighted: ", plab)) +
  geom_smooth(data = filter(gene_break_summary,functional_category == plab),
              aes(x = avg_length, y = n_breaks), method = "lm",
              formula = y ~ x, se = TRUE, inherit.aes = FALSE, color = "black", size = 0.9)


# table annotating permutation p-values (BH and FWER)
annot_tbl <- obs_diff_df %>%
  left_join(emp_p_df, by = "pathway") %>%
  arrange(p_emp_BH) %>%
  mutate(p_emp_BH = signif(p_emp_BH, 3), p_fwer_perm = signif(p_fwer_perm, 3)) %>%
  dplyr::select(pathway, obs_diff, p_emp_BH, p_fwer_perm) %>%
  arrange(p_emp_BH)

ptable <- gridExtra::tableGrob(annot_tbl, rows = NULL, theme = gridExtra::ttheme_default(base_size = 8))

ltable <- gridExtra::tableGrob(logit_results, rows = NULL, theme = gridExtra::ttheme_default(base_size = 8))



ptable_plot <- ggdraw(ptable)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir,"ptable.png"), ptable_plot,
       width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

ltable_plot <- ggdraw(ltable)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir,"Ltable.png"), ltable_plot,
       width = 8, height = 4, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

right_col <- p_slope + inset_element(ptable, left = 0.02, bottom = 0.60, right = 0.98, top = 0.98)
right_col + pC_inset + inset_element(ltable, left = 0.02, bottom = 0.60, right = 0.98, top = 0.98)


write.csv(
  global_category_anova,
  file = file.path(output_dir, "global_category_anova.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

logit_results <- logit_results %>%
  rename(
    category = pathway
  )

write.csv(
  logit_results,
  file = file.path(output_dir, "logit_results.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

```




<!-- ## Supplemental -->

<!-- ## HyPhy: BUSTED Gene-Wide selection test (not considering independent branches separate) -->
<!-- Note: BUSTED is a gene-wide metric--- we dont get independent branch level resolution. -->
<!-- e.g. The test for selection (pos/neg/neut) is overall, gene-wide... We can pull evidence ratios for site-lvl info -->
<!-- BUT these are not to be taken as statistical support for selection at a specific site. -->
<!-- Here, we opt to focus on signals of any positive selection across any branch FOR each individual tree (which happens to be what BUSTED's advertised strength is to do...).  -->

<!-- ```{r, BUSTED, COMPLETE} -->

<!-- ## we started with busted... it's a gene wide metric so we dont get species/branch level resolution but rather estimate... -->
<!-- ## e.g. for this gene, do we anticipate negative/positive or null selection... -->

<!-- #write in all the good stuff -->
<!-- busted_res <- read.csv("tbusted_summary.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) -->
<!-- function_metadata <- read_csv("gene_sets_union.csv") -->
<!-- busted_res <- merge(busted_res, function_metadata, by.x = "gene", by.y = "symbol", all.x = TRUE) -->
<!-- gene_length <- read_tsv("gene_lengths.tsv")%>% -->
<!--   mutate(gene_id = toupper(gene_id)) -->
<!-- gene_length <- gene_length %>% group_by(gene_dir) %>%  -->
<!--   mutate(avg_length = mean(seq_length, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% dplyr::select(c(gene_dir, avg_length)) -->
<!-- busted_res <- merge(busted_res, gene_length, by.x = "gene", by.y = "gene_dir") %>% unique() -->

<!-- #busted_res %>% pull(partition) %>% table() -->
<!-- #busted_res <- busted_res %>% filter(partition == 0)  -->



<!-- ###### Knobs for turniong -->
<!-- min_prop_pos <- 0.05      # minimum breadth to consider (0.5%) -->
<!-- top_n_genes <- 10 -->
<!-- omega_thresh <- 1 -->
<!-- omega_cap <- 100 -->
<!-- n_tests <- busted_res %>% distinct(gene) %>% nrow() -->

<!-- busted_res <- busted_res %>% -->
<!--   rename(functional_category = pathway_label) -->


<!-- busted_res <- filter_one2manyID(busted_res, "gene") -->

<!-- busted_res <- exorcise(busted_res, "gene") -->

<!-- ####  numeric-izing where neeeded -->
<!-- busted_clean <- busted_res %>% -->
<!--   mutate( -->
<!--     pval                     = as.numeric(pval), -->
<!--     lrt                      = as.numeric(lrt), -->
<!--     omega_positive           = as.numeric(omega_positive), -->
<!--     omega_neutral            = as.numeric(omega_neutral), -->
<!--     omega_purifying          = as.numeric(omega_purifying), -->
<!--     proportion_positive      = as.numeric(proportion_positive), -->
<!--     proportion_neutral       = as.numeric(proportion_neutral), -->
<!--     proportion_purifying     = as.numeric(proportion_purifying), -->
<!--     prop_pos_ok              = if_else(!is.na(proportion_positive) & proportion_positive >= min_prop_pos, TRUE, FALSE), -->
<!--     prop_pur_ok              = if_else(!is.na(proportion_purifying) & proportion_purifying >= min_prop_pos, TRUE, FALSE), -->
<!--     branches_clean           = str_remove_all(branches %||% "", "^\\[|\\]$") %>% str_replace_all('\"', ""), -->
<!--     number_of_sequences_num  = ifelse(branches_clean == "", NA_integer_, str_count(branches_clean, ",\\s*") + 1) -->
<!--   ) %>% -->
<!--   #BONF CORRECT -->
<!--   mutate( -->
<!--     pval_for_adj = ifelse(is.na(pval), NA_real_, ifelse(pval <= 0, .Machine$double.xmin, pval)), -->
<!--     p_bonf = pmin(1, pval_for_adj * n_tests), -->
<!--     busted_signif = !is.na(p_bonf) & p_bonf <= 0.05 -->
<!--   ) %>% -->
<!--   filter(!is.na( functional_category ) & omega_positive < 100 & omega_neutral < 100 & omega_purifying < 100) -->

<!-- ##### settingPOS RULES -->
<!-- pos_qual <- busted_clean %>% -->
<!--   filter( -->
<!--     !is.na(pval) & pval < 0.05, -->
<!--     !is.na(p_bonf) & p_bonf < 0.05, -->
<!--     !is.na(omega_positive) & omega_positive > omega_thresh, -->
<!--     !is.na(omega_neutral)  & omega_neutral  <= 1, -->
<!--     !is.na(omega_purifying) & omega_purifying <= omega_neutral, -->
<!--     prop_pos_ok == TRUE -->
<!--   ) -->
<!-- ##### settingNEG RULES -->
<!-- pur_qual <- busted_clean %>% -->
<!--   filter( -->
<!--     !is.na(pval) & pval < 0.05, -->
<!--     !is.na(p_bonf) & p_bonf < 0.05, -->
<!--     !is.na(omega_purifying) & omega_purifying > omega_thresh, -->
<!--     !is.na(omega_positive) & omega_positive <= omega_neutral, -->
<!--     !is.na(omega_neutral)  & omega_neutral  <= 1, -->
<!--     prop_pur_ok == TRUE -->
<!--   ) -->
<!-- ###### selecting top_n_genes per pos/pur -->
<!-- top_pos <- pos_qual %>% -->
<!--   group_by( functional_category ) %>% -->
<!--   slice_max(proportion_positive, n = top_n_genes, with_ties = FALSE) %>% -->
<!--   mutate(subgroup = "POS") %>% -->
<!--   ungroup() -->
<!-- top_pur <- pur_qual %>% -->
<!--   group_by( functional_category ) %>% -->
<!--   slice_max(proportion_purifying, n = top_n_genes, with_ties = FALSE) %>% -->
<!--   mutate(subgroup = "PUR") %>% -->
<!--   ungroup() -->
<!-- #bring em together -->
<!-- top_union <- bind_rows(top_pos, top_pur) %>% -->
<!--   distinct(gene,functional_category, .keep_all = TRUE) -->
<!-- #POS"PUR ORDERING -->
<!-- top_union <- top_union %>% -->
<!--   group_by( functional_category ) %>% -->
<!--   arrange( -->
<!--     factor(subgroup, levels = c("POS", "PUR")), -->
<!--     desc(if_else(subgroup == "POS", proportion_positive, proportion_purifying)) -->
<!--   ) %>% -->
<!--   mutate(order_within_pathway = row_number()) %>% -->
<!--   ungroup() %>% -->
<!--   mutate( -->
<!--     gene_label = paste0("[", subgroup, "] ", gene), -->
<!--     gene_ord   = reorder_within(gene_label, -order_within_pathway,functional_category) -->
<!--   ) -->
<!-- #avg genne length scale -->
<!-- busted_long <- top_union %>% -->
<!--   dplyr::select(gene_label, gene_ord,functional_category, -->
<!--          proportion_purifying, proportion_neutral, proportion_positive, -->
<!--          avg_length) %>% -->
<!--   pivot_longer(starts_with("proportion_"), -->
<!--                names_to = "sel_class", -->
<!--                values_to = "proportion") %>% -->
<!--   mutate( -->
<!--     sel_class = recode(sel_class, -->
<!--       proportion_purifying = "Purifying", -->
<!--       proportion_neutral   = "Neutral", -->
<!--       proportion_positive  = "Positive" -->
<!--     ), -->
<!--     class_count = proportion * avg_length -->
<!--   ) -->
<!-- ####### PANEL A -->
<!-- pA <- busted_long %>% -->
<!--   ggplot(aes(gene_ord, class_count, fill = sel_class)) + -->
<!--   geom_col() + -->
<!--   coord_flip() + -->
<!--   facet_wrap(~functional_category, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) + -->
<!--   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") + -->
<!--   scale_x_reordered() + -->
<!--   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) + -->
<!--   labs( -->
<!--     x = NULL, -->
<!--     y = "Estimated codons (avg gene length × site proportion)", -->
<!--     title = glue( -->
<!--       "BUSTED selection regime composition — top {top_n_genes} positive and purifying genes per pathway" -->
<!--     ), -->
<!--     subtitle = -->
<!--       glue("Genes ranked within pathways: POS = highest proportion of ω>{omega_thresh} sites; PUR = highest proportion of ω<{omega_thresh} sites. Bars scaled by average gene length."), -->
<!--     fill = "Selection class" -->
<!--   ) + -->
<!--   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) + -->
<!--   theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8)) -->

<!-- ########## PANEL B -->
<!-- tiny <- 1e-6 -->
<!-- plot_top_pos_per_pathway <- 50 -->

<!-- pos_pool <- top_pos %>% -->
<!--   filter(!is.na(omega_positive), omega_positive > 1, prop_pos_ok == TRUE) -->
<!-- top_pos_for_plot <- pos_pool %>% -->
<!--   group_by( functional_category ) %>% -->
<!--   slice_max(order_by = proportion_positive, n = plot_top_pos_per_pathway, with_ties = FALSE) %>% -->
<!--   ungroup() %>% -->
<!--   distinct(gene,functional_category, .keep_all = TRUE) -->
<!-- top_keys_pos <- top_pos_for_plot %>% distinct(gene,functional_category) -->

<!-- # 4) Build plotting table: keep only these genes (consistent set), compute plotting variables -->
<!-- cands <- busted_clean %>% -->
<!--   semi_join(top_keys_pos, by = c("gene", "functional_category")) %>% -->
<!--   mutate( -->
<!--     omega_pos_plot  = pmin(omega_positive, omega_cap), -->
<!--     omega_pos_value = pmax(omega_positive, tiny) -->
<!--   ) -->
<!-- label_df_all <- cands %>% filter(omega_pos_value > omega_thresh) -->

<!-- #### ADJUS t duplicated names -->
<!-- dup_genes <- cands %>% count(gene) %>% filter(n > 1) %>% pull(gene) -->
<!-- #non-dupes path color -->
<!-- label_df_nondup <- label_df_all %>% filter(!gene %in% dup_genes) -->
<!-- ### dupes get black labels -->
<!-- label_df_dup <- label_df_all %>% -->
<!--   filter(gene %in% dup_genes) %>% -->
<!--   group_by(gene) %>% -->
<!--   slice_max(order_by = omega_pos_value, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>% -->
<!--   ungroup() -->
<!-- pB <- ggplot(cands, aes(x = proportion_positive, y = omega_pos_plot, -->
<!--                         color =functional_category, size = number_of_sequences_num)) + -->
<!--   geom_point(alpha = 0.9) + -->
<!--   geom_hline(yintercept = omega_thresh, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") + -->
<!--   annotate("text", x = Inf, y = omega_thresh, label = paste0("ω = ", omega_thresh), -->
<!--            hjust = 1.05, vjust = -0.5, size = 3) + -->
<!--   # colored labels for single-pathway genes -->
<!--   geom_text_repel(data = label_df_nondup, -->
<!--                   aes(label = gene), -->
<!--                   size = 3, -->
<!--                   max.overlaps = Inf, -->
<!--                   box.padding = 0.3, -->
<!--                   point.padding = 0.2, -->
<!--                   segment.color = "grey50", -->
<!--                   show.legend = FALSE) + -->
<!--   # black labels for duplicated genes (one label per gene) -->
<!--   geom_text_repel(data = label_df_dup, -->
<!--                   aes(x = proportion_positive, y = omega_pos_plot, label = gene), -->
<!--                   color = "black", -->
<!--                   size = 3, -->
<!--                   max.overlaps = Inf, -->
<!--                   box.padding = 0.3, -->
<!--                   point.padding = 0.2, -->
<!--                   segment.color = "grey50", -->
<!--                   show.legend = FALSE, -->
<!--                   inherit.aes = FALSE) + -->
<!--   scale_y_continuous(name = expression(omega[3])) + -->
<!--   labs( -->
<!--     x = "Proportion of sites in ω3 (p3)", -->
<!--     title = "Extent vs intensity of positive selection (BUSTED)", -->
<!--     subtitle = paste0("Plot shows top ", plot_top_pos_per_pathway, -->
<!--                       " positively-selected genes per pathway (ω > 1); genes with ω > ", omega_thresh, " are labelled."), -->
<!--     color = "Biological process", -->
<!--     size  = "Total branches" -->
<!--   ) + -->
<!--   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) -->
<!-- pA -->
<!-- #################### CRITCAL NOTE ON INTERPRETATION... -->
<!-- # Proportions reflect BUSTED model–inferred mixture weights across selection regimes and should be interpreted as relative regime composition rather than literal fractions of sites under selection. -->
<!-- ### never claim where selection occurred, only that it occurred somewhere on the tree. -->

<!-- ``` -->






<!-- ## HyPhy: FUBAR Gene-Wide selection test (not considering independent branches separate) -->
<!-- NOTE: as with the previous models FUBAR tells us: -->
<!-- about selection dynamics PER GENE/across the entire multiple seq alignment...  -->
<!-- NOT per branch/per independent sequence from a given species... -->
<!-- Panel A, n = raw count of codon under pos selection; proportion x-axis refers to percent of gene total length undergoing pos selection -->
<!-- Panel B, gray background bars reflect gene length -->
<!-- Panel C, high intensity is relative but generally, strong = > 5 to 10+. Really high intensity might suggest host-pathogen dynamics where host has to evolve faster in response to some immune/or OTHER metabolic threat... -->

<!-- ```{r} -->

<!-- #### FUBAR tells us about selection dynamics per gene... not per branch/per independent sequence -->
<!-- fubar_res <- read.csv("tfubar_summary.csv") -->
<!-- #### original -->
<!-- #### program,gene,branch,GTR,sites,alpha,beta,beta-alpha,Prob[alpha>beta],Prob[alpha<beta],BayesFactor[alpha<beta] -->
<!-- #### let's adjust -->

<!-- function_metadata <- read_csv("gene_sets_union.csv") -->

<!-- ## merges metadata to FUBAR data but also drops branch-level info since it was repetitive (given we only have data per gene-- e.g. across all branches) -->
<!-- fubar_res <- merge(fubar_res, function_metadata, by.x = "gene", by.y = "symbol", all.x = TRUE)  -->

<!-- fubar_res <- rename(fubar_res, -->
<!--                     beta_minus_alpha = beta.alpha, -->
<!--                     P_alphaGTbeta = Prob.alpha.beta., -->
<!--                     P_betaGTalpha = Prob.alpha.beta..1, -->
<!--                     BayesFactor_betaGTalpha = BayesFactor.alpha.beta., -->
<!--                   functional_category= pathway_label ) -->
<!-- fubar_res$branch <- gsub("\\|", "_", fubar_res$branch) -->
<!-- gene_length <- read_tsv("gene_lengths.tsv") %>% -->
<!--   mutate(gene_id = toupper(gene_id)) -->
<!-- fubar_res <- merge(fubar_res, gene_length, by.x = "branch", by.y = "gene_id") -->
<!-- fubar_res <- fubar_res %>% filter(!str_detect(branch, "Node|root")) -->
<!-- fubar_res <- fubar_res %>% group_by(gene) %>% -->
<!--   mutate(avg_length = mean(seq_length, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% -->
<!--   ungroup() %>% dplyr::select(-c(branch, fasta_file, GTR, seq_length)) %>% unique()  -->
<!-- ######### see we only have a certain select set of branches represented in any given partition: -->
<!-- #fubar_res %>% filter(gene_id == "AACS" & partition == 0) %>% View() -->
<!-- ######### we should be adjusting from here to add in the metadata on EQ/diet to make sure we select partitions which have equitable across-group representation... -->
<!-- ########## LETS START by just using a single partition so we know we are at least not biasing in terms of recombination events (altho we still definitely are, as mentioned above, by species (e.g. diet/eq group) representation...)... should write a filter/check for that up here... -->
<!-- ### TRYING to ballpark #ofbranches per partition... -->
<!-- #fubar_res %>% pull(partition) %>% table() -->

<!-- #fubar_res <- fubar_res %>% filter(partition == 0) -->

<!-- ##################TUNABLE VARS TO START -->
<!-- #bayesian posterior prob for calling pos site -->
<!-- fubar_pp_thresh <- 0.90 -->
<!-- site_cols <- colnames(fubar_res) -->


<!-- fubar_res <- filter_one2manyID(fubar_res, "gene") -->


<!-- fubar_res <- exorcise(fubar_res, "gene") -->

<!-- #expanding columns for site info access -->
<!-- # parse site vectors (robust) and flag pos/neg sites -->
<!-- fubar_sites <- fubar_res %>% -->
<!--   mutate( -->
<!--     across( -->
<!--       c( -->
<!--         sites, alpha, beta, beta_minus_alpha, -->
<!--         P_alphaGTbeta, P_betaGTalpha, BayesFactor_betaGTalpha -->
<!--       ), -->
<!--       ~ { -->
<!--         x <- as.character(.x) -->
<!--         x <- str_remove_all(x, "^\\s*\\[|\\]\\s*$") -->
<!--         x <- str_replace_all(x, "\\s*'\\s*|\\s*\"\\s*", "") -->
<!--         str_trim(x) -->
<!--       } -->
<!--     ) -->
<!--   ) %>% -->
<!--   separate_rows( -->
<!--     sites, alpha, beta, beta_minus_alpha, -->
<!--     P_alphaGTbeta, P_betaGTalpha, BayesFactor_betaGTalpha, -->
<!--     sep = ",\\s*", convert = FALSE -->
<!--   ) %>% -->
<!--   mutate( -->
<!--     site = as.integer(sites), -->
<!--     alpha = as.numeric(alpha), -->
<!--     beta = as.numeric(beta), -->
<!--     beta_minus_alpha2 = beta - alpha, -->
<!--     pp_pos = as.numeric(P_betaGTalpha), -->
<!--     pp_neg = as.numeric(P_alphaGTbeta), -->
<!--     bf_pos = as.numeric(BayesFactor_betaGTalpha), -->
<!--     avg_length = as.numeric(avg_length), -->

<!--     ## explicit significant positive-site definition used everywhere -->
<!--     is_sig_pos_site = !is.na(pp_pos) & pp_pos >= fubar_pp_thresh & beta_minus_alpha2 > 1, -->
<!--     is_sig_neg_site = !is.na(pp_neg) & pp_neg >= fubar_pp_thresh & beta_minus_alpha2 < -1, -->

<!--     omega_site = pmin(beta / pmax(alpha, tiny), omega_cap), -->
<!--     size_metric = pmax(omega_site, 1.01) -->
<!--   ) %>% -->
<!--   filter(!is.na(site), !is.na( functional_category )) -->

<!-- ###gene lvl summ for breadth/intensity -->
<!-- fubar_gene <- fubar_sites %>% -->
<!--   group_by(gene) %>% -->
<!--   summarise( -->
<!--    functional_category = first(na.omit( functional_category )), -->
<!--     avg_length = first(na.omit(avg_length)), -->
<!--     n_sites_reported = n_distinct(site), -->
<!--     n_pos_sites = sum(is_sig_pos_site, na.rm = TRUE), -->
<!--     n_neg_sites = sum(is_sig_neg_site, na.rm = TRUE), -->
<!--     prop_pos = ifelse(!is.na(avg_length) & avg_length > 0, -->
<!--                       n_pos_sites / avg_length, NA_real_), -->
<!--     median_beta_minus_alpha = ifelse(n_pos_sites > 0, -->
<!--                                      median(beta_minus_alpha2[is_sig_pos_site], na.rm = TRUE), -->
<!--                                      NA_real_), -->
<!--     mean_beta_minus_alpha = ifelse(n_pos_sites > 0, -->
<!--                                    mean(beta_minus_alpha2[is_sig_pos_site], na.rm = TRUE), -->
<!--                                    NA_real_), -->
<!--     max_beta_minus_alpha = ifelse(n_pos_sites > 0, -->
<!--                                   max(beta_minus_alpha2[is_sig_pos_site], na.rm = TRUE), -->
<!--                                   NA_real_), -->
<!--     median_pp_pos = ifelse(n_pos_sites > 0, -->
<!--                         median(pp_pos[is_sig_pos_site], na.rm = TRUE), -->
<!--                         NA_real_), -->
<!--     p90_beta = ifelse(n_pos_sites > 0, -->
<!--                       quantile(beta[is_sig_pos_site], 0.9, na.rm = TRUE), -->
<!--                       NA_real_), -->
<!--     mean_pp_pos = ifelse(n_pos_sites > 0, -->
<!--                          mean(pp_pos[is_sig_pos_site], na.rm = TRUE), -->
<!--                          NA_real_), -->
<!--     .groups = "drop" -->
<!--   ) -->


<!-- ########PLOTTING -->
<!-- pA_data <- fubar_gene %>% -->
<!--   filter(!is.na(n_pos_sites) & n_pos_sites > 0 & -->
<!--            mean_beta_minus_alpha > 5 & median_pp_pos > 0.9) %>% -->

<!--                                              NA_real_)) -->

<!-- pA <-   ggplot(pA_data, aes(x = prop_pos, y = mean_beta_minus_alpha_cap)) + -->

<!--   labs( -->

<!--   ) + -->
<!--   theme_bw(base_size = 11) -->

<!-- #PANEL B: Top 10 Genes by Breadth (Prop Positive) --- -->
<!-- # site-level plotting: only positive sites (pp >= 0.9) and bf finite -->
<!-- top_n_per_pathway <- 5 -->
<!-- top_genes_by_pathway <- fubar_sites %>% -->
<!--   filter(!is.na( functional_category )) %>% -->
<!--   filter((pp_pos >= fubar_pp_thresh & beta_minus_alpha2 > 1 & bf_pos > 20) | -->

<!--   summarise(max_mag = max(abs(beta_minus_alpha2), na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>% -->

<!--   slice_max(order_by = max_mag, n = top_n_per_pathway, with_ties = FALSE) %>% -->
<!--   pull(gene) -->
<!-- top_list <- unique(top_genes_by_pathway) -->

<!-- b_data <- fubar_sites %>% -->
<!--   filter(gene %in% top_list) %>% -->

<!-- # sup_B_pos <- p_forest( -->
<!-- #   fubar_path_pos %>% mutate(p_adj = p_adj, sig = sig), -->
