<!-- ``` -->





<!-- ## HyPhy: FEL Gene-Wide selection test  -->
<!-- Fel does not consider independent branches separate (e.g. gene-wide + site-level only) -->

<!-- ```{r} -->

<!-- #thresholding vars options -->
<!-- fel_p_thresh <- 0.05 -->
<!-- top_n_per_pathway <- 10 -->
<!-- top_n_genes <- 13 -->

<!-- fel_res <- read.csv("tfel_summary.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) -->
<!-- function_metadata <- read_csv("gene_sets_union.csv") -->
<!-- fel_res <- merge(fel_res, function_metadata, by.x = "gene_id", by.y = "symbol", all.x = TRUE)  -->
<!-- gene_length <- read_tsv("gene_lengths.tsv") %>% -->
<!--   mutate(gene_id = toupper(gene_id)) -->
<!-- fel_res <- merge(fel_res, gene_length, by.x = "branch_id", by.y = "gene_id")  -->
<!-- fel_res <- fel_res %>% filter(!str_detect(branch_id, "Node|root")) -->
<!-- ######### opting to leave branch info in... this will help us keep track of "who" was included per partition -->
<!-- fel_res <- fel_res %>% dplyr::select(-c( fasta_file)) %>% unique() %>% group_by(gene_id) %>% -->
<!--   mutate(avg_length = mean(seq_length, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% -->
<!--   ungroup() %>% dplyr::select(-c(seq_length)) %>% unique()  -->
<!-- ######### see we only have a certain select set of branches represented in any given partition: -->
<!-- #fel_res %>% filter(gene_id == "AACS" & partition == 0) %>% View() -->
<!-- ######### we should be adjusting from here to add in the metadata on EQ/diet to make sure we select partitions which have equitable across-group representation... -->
<!-- ########## LETS START by just using a single partition so we know we are at least not biasing in terms of recombination events (altho we still definitely are, as mentioned above, by species (e.g. diet/eq group) representation...)... should write a filter/check for that up here... -->
<!-- ### TRYING to ballpark #ofbranches per partition... -->
<!-- fel_res %>% pull(partition) %>% table() -->

<!-- #fel_res <- fel_res %>% filter(partition == 0) -->
<!-- ########### ... or maybe we don't have to filter partitioned data... maybe as long as we know everything HAS been partitioned, we can trudge ahead knowing that all input comes from regions that are inferred not to be recombining... BUT I ACTUALLY THINK we can't do this because each partition has a completely different set of branches/seqs represented... across partitions we'll have multiple hits for multiple seqs... some may be the same branches? but it would be invalid to use this as validation since the observation is in relation to completely different phylogeny/rest of branches associated with the partition -->



<!-- ### pull site level cols -->
<!-- site_pos_candidates <- c("FEL_omega_coord", "FEL_LRTs_site", "FEL_sig_sites") -->
<!-- pos_col <- intersect(site_pos_candidates, colnames(fel_res)) %>% first() -->
<!-- site_cols <- c("FEL_sig_sites", "FEL_site_pvalues", "FEL_site_omegas", -->
<!--                "FEL_site_LRTs", "FEL_site_classes", "FEL_omega_coord", "FEL_LRTs_site") -->
<!-- site_cols <- site_cols[site_cols %in% colnames(fel_res)] -->


<!-- fel_res <- filter_one2manyID(fel_res, "gene_id") -->


<!-- fel_res <- exorcise(fel_res, "gene_id") -->

<!-- ### Parsing those site level info -->
<!-- fel_sites <- fel_res %>% -->
<!--   # clean list-like columns (strip brackets/quotes) and explode rows -->
<!--   mutate(across(all_of(site_cols), ~ { -->
<!--     s <- as.character(.x) -->
<!--     s <- str_remove_all(s, "^\\s*\\[|\\]\\s*$") -->
<!--     s <- str_replace_all(s, "\\s*'\\s*|\\s*\"\\s*", "") -->
<!--     s <- str_trim(s) -->
<!--     ifelse(s == "" | s == "NA", NA_character_, s) -->
<!--   })) %>% -->
<!--   tidyr::separate_rows(all_of(site_cols), sep = ",\\s*", convert = FALSE) %>% -->
<!-- # inside your pipeline, replace the numeric coercion lines with these -->
<!-- mutate( -->
<!--   site = coalesce( -->
<!--     suppressWarnings(as.numeric(!!sym(pos_col))), -->
<!--     suppressWarnings(as.numeric(FEL_sig_sites)), -->
<!--     suppressWarnings(as.numeric(FEL_LRTs_site)) -->
<!--   ), -->
<!--   # robust numeric parsing for pvalue & omega -->
<!--   pvalue = as.numeric(FEL_site_pvalues) %>% -->
<!--     as.character() %>% -->
<!--     str_replace_all("\u2212", "-") %>%                        # unicode minus -> ascii -->
<!--     str_replace_all("\\s+", "") %>%                           # remove stray inner spaces -->
<!--     str_replace_all("(,)(?=\\d{3}(?:[^\\d]|$))", "") %>%      # remove thousands separators like 1,000 (optional) -->
<!--     str_replace_all("[^0-9eE+\\-\\.]", "") %>%                # keep only numeric token chars -->
<!--     na_if("") %>% -->
<!--     as.numeric(), -->
<!--   omega = as.numeric(FEL_site_omegas) %>% -->
<!--     as.character() %>% -->
<!--     str_replace_all("\u2212", "-") %>% -->
<!--     str_replace_all("\\s+", "") %>% -->
<!--     str_replace_all("(,)(?=\\d{3}(?:[^\\d]|$))", "") %>%      # remove thousands separators if present -->
<!--     str_replace_all("[^0-9eE+\\-\\.]", "") %>% -->
<!--     na_if("") %>% -->
<!--     as.numeric(), -->
<!--   lrt = FEL_site_LRTs %>% -->
<!--     as.character() %>% -->
<!--     str_replace_all("\u2212", "-") %>% -->
<!--     str_replace_all("\\s+", "") %>% -->
<!--     str_replace_all("[^0-9eE+\\-\\.]", "") %>% -->
<!--     na_if("") %>% -->
<!--     as.numeric(), -->
<!--   site_class = FEL_site_classes %>% -->
<!--     str_remove_all("^\\[|\\]$") %>% -->
<!--     str_replace_all("^'|'$|^\"|\"$", "") %>% -->
<!--     str_trim() -->
<!--   ) %>% -->
<!--   filter(!is.na(site)) %>% -->
<!--   mutate(site_class = tolower(site_class)) %>% -->
<!--   distinct(gene_id, site, .keep_all = TRUE) %>% -->
<!--   mutate( -->
<!--     is_sig = if_else(!is.na(pvalue) & pvalue <= fel_p_thresh, TRUE, FALSE), -->
<!--     is_pos_site = (site_class == "positive" & is_sig), -->
<!--     is_neg_site = (site_class == "negative" & is_sig), -->
<!--     size_metric = if_else(!is.na(pvalue), pmax(-log10(pvalue), 0.001), NA_real_) -->
<!--   ) -->

<!-- # If some genes didn't pick up total_codons via fel_res, try pulling from fel_sites/ gene_length if available -->
<!-- # prefer first non-NA per gene -->
<!-- gene_total_codons <- fel_res %>% -->
<!--   dplyr::select(gene_id, FEL_n_sites, avg_length) %>% -->
<!--   distinct() %>% -->
<!--   group_by(gene_id) %>% -->
<!--   summarise(FEL_n_sites = first(na.omit(FEL_n_sites)), .groups = "drop") -->

<!-- # join to fel_sites so site-level rows have gene lengths available -->
<!-- fel_sites <- fel_sites %>% left_join(gene_total_codons, by = "gene_id") -->

<!-- fel_sites <- fel_sites %>% -->

<!-- ## HyPhy: MEME Seq-/Branch-wide Test for selection across genes perfunctional_category/biological class -->
<!-- NOTE: this is primarily  a SITE-LEVEL METRIC... \n -->
<!-- we can get and show the branch level data for a given site but we should really be looking for where we have a site repeatedly observed as under selection across MULTIPLE branches... \n -->
<!-- Try looking for either tree wide or among a species-level group. -->

<!-- ```{r} -->

<!-- meme_res <- read.csv("tmeme_summary.csv") -->
<!-- #colnames(meme_res) -->
<!-- function_metadata <- read_csv("gene_sets_union.csv") -->
<!-- meme_res <- merge(meme_res, function_metadata, by.x = "gene", by.y = "symbol") -->
<!-- #### remove internal/root branches... for clarity/interpretations -->
<!-- meme_res <- meme_res %>% filter(!str_detect(branch, "NODE|root")) -->
<!-- #see python aux script for which we used to handle gene length consideration... -->
<!-- #/projects/b1057/apulvino/Chapter2/OrthologExplorer/results_sequences... rest of work for the entire proj is in v4 subdir... -->
<!-- #used after so we have clean merge with fel_res df: sed -i 's/|/_/g' gene_lengths.tsv -->
<!-- gene_length <- read_tsv("gene_lengths.tsv") %>% -->
<!--   mutate(gene_id = toupper(gene_id)) -->
<!-- meme_res <- merge(meme_res, gene_length, by.x = "branch" , by.y = "gene_id") %>% unique() -->
<!-- meme_res$species <- sub(".*_([A-Za-z]+_[A-Za-z]+)_.*", "\\1", meme_res$branch) -->


<!-- meme_res <- filter_one2manyID(meme_res, "gene") -->

<!-- meme_res <- exorcise(meme_res, "gene") -->

<!-- #meme_res <- meme_res %>% filter(partition == 0) -->
<!-- # --------------------------- -->
<!-- # config -->
<!-- PVAL_THRESHOLD <- 0.05 -->
<!-- TOP_N_GENES_BY_PATHWAY <- 10 -->
<!-- TOP_N_GENES_FOR_PANELB <- 5 -->
<!-- INTENSITY_FUNC <- function(beta, alpha) { -->
<!--   # avoid division by zero; small floor -->
<!--   alpha2 <- ifelse(is.na(alpha) | alpha <= 0, NA_real_, alpha) -->
<!--   beta2  <- ifelse(is.na(beta), NA_real_, beta) -->
<!--   beta2 / alpha2 -->
<!-- } -->

<!-- # --------------------------- -->
<!-- # helper parsers for your string-list columns -->
<!-- # Accepts inputs like: -->
<!-- #  "[539, 542, 548]"  or "['GGG', 'TTC']" or "[0.001, 0.05]" -->
<!-- # Returns numeric vector (for numeric columns) or character vector (for substitutions) -->
<!-- parse_numeric_list <- function(s) { -->
<!--   if (is.null(s) || is.na(s)) return(numeric(0)) -->
<!--   if (is.list(s)) { -->
<!--     # already parsed -->
<!--     v <- unlist(s) -->
<!--     return(as.numeric(v)) -->
<!--   } -->
<!--   # string: remove surrounding brackets and whitespace -->
<!--   st <- as.character(s) -->
<!--   st <- str_trim(st) -->
<!--   st <- str_replace_all(st, "^\\[\\s*|\\s*\\]$", "") -->
<!--   if (st == "") return(numeric(0)) -->
<!--   parts <- str_split(st, ",")[[1]] %>% str_trim() -->
<!--   # remove possible quotes -->
<!--   parts <- str_replace_all(parts, "^['\"]|['\"]$", "") -->
<!--   # convert to numeric; drop non-numerics -->
<!--   nums <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(parts)) -->
<!--   nums[!is.na(nums)] -->
<!-- } -->

<!-- parse_char_list <- function(s) { -->
<!--   if (is.null(s) || is.na(s)) return(character(0)) -->
<!--   if (is.list(s)) { -->
<!--     v <- unlist(s) -->
<!--     return(as.character(v)) -->
<!--   } -->
<!--   st <- as.character(s) -->
<!--   st <- str_trim(st) -->
<!--   st <- str_replace_all(st, "^\\[\\s*|\\s*\\]$", "") -->
<!--   if (st == "") return(character(0)) -->
<!--   parts <- str_split(st, ",")[[1]] %>% str_trim() -->
<!--   # remove surrounding quotes maybe present -->
<!--   parts <- str_replace_all(parts, "^['\"]|['\"]$", "") -->
<!--   parts -->
<!-- } -->


<!-- # --------------------------- -->
<!-- # create list-columns parsed on the fly, but keep meme_res compact -->
<!-- meme_parsed <- meme_res %>% -->
<!--   mutate( -->
<!--     .site_pos = map(site_positions, parse_numeric_list), -->
<!--     .site_pval = map(site_pval, parse_numeric_list), -->
<!--     .site_LRT = map(site_LRT, parse_numeric_list), -->
<!--     .site_alpha = map(site_alpha, parse_numeric_list), -->
<!--     .site_beta_plus = map(site_beta_plus, parse_numeric_list), -->
<!--     .site_MEMElogl = map(site_MEMElogl, parse_numeric_list), -->
<!--     .site_sub = map(site_substitution, parse_char_list) -->
<!--   ) -->

<!-- # defensive alignment: ensure vectors align by truncating to common length per row -->
<!-- meme_parsed <- meme_parsed %>% -->
<!--   mutate( -->
<!--     .n_sites = map_int(.site_pos, length), -->
<!--     # apply alignment: truncate all lists to the minimum positive length across relevant lists -->
<!--     .aligned = pmap(list(.site_pos, .site_pval, .site_alpha, .site_beta_plus, .site_LRT, .site_MEMElogl, .site_sub), -->
<!--                     function(pos, pval, alpha, beta, lrt, memelog, sub) { -->
<!--                       lens <- c(length(pos), length(pval), length(alpha), length(beta), length(lrt), length(memelog), length(sub)) -->
<!--                       # if everything empty -> return empty lists -->
<!--                       if (all(lens == 0)) { -->
<!--                         return(tibble(pos = numeric(0), pval = numeric(0), alpha = numeric(0), -->
<!--                                       beta = numeric(0), lrt = numeric(0), memelog = numeric(0), sub = character(0))) -->
<!--                       } -->
<!--                       nmin <- min(lens[lens > 0])  # prefer positive lengths -->
<!--                       if (is.infinite(nmin)) nmin <- 0 -->
<!--                       # slice safely (if some are shorter than nmin use their length) -->
<!--                       pos2 <- if (length(pos) >= nmin) pos[1:nmin] else numeric(0) -->
<!--                       pval2 <- if (length(pval) >= nmin) pval[1:nmin] else numeric(0) -->
<!--                       alpha2 <- if (length(alpha) >= nmin) alpha[1:nmin] else numeric(0) -->
<!--                       beta2 <- if (length(beta) >= nmin) beta[1:nmin] else numeric(0) -->
<!--                       lrt2 <- if (length(lrt) >= nmin) lrt[1:nmin] else numeric(0) -->
<!--                       memelog2 <- if (length(memelog) >= nmin) memelog[1:nmin] else numeric(0) -->
<!--                       sub2 <- if (length(sub) >= nmin) sub[1:nmin] else character(0) -->
<!--                       tibble(pos = pos2, pval = pval2, alpha = alpha2, beta = beta2, lrt = lrt2, memelog = memelog2, sub = sub2) -->
<!--                     }) -->
<!--   ) -->

<!-- # --------------------------- -->
<!-- # Gene-level aggregation (unique positions per gene) -->
<!-- # We'll compute: -->
<!-- #   n_pos_unique = number unique codons across all rows for that gene where .pos_flag==TRUE -->
<!-- #   prop_pos = n_pos_unique / seq_length -->
<!-- #   mean_intensity = mean(log10(intensity)) over unique positive positions (or NA if none) -->
<!-- #   max_intensity = max(intensity) among positive positions -->
<!-- # Also gather per-gene vector of unique positions and intensities for panel B/D use. -->

<!--   group_by( functional_category , group.x, group.y, gene, seq_length) %>% -->
<!--   summarise( -->
<!--     all_pos = list(flatten_dbl(.pos_vec)), -->
<!--     all_flags = list(flatten_lgl(.pos_flag)), -->
<!--     all_int  = list(flatten_dbl(.intensity)), -->
<!--     # <<< collect all species across rows contributing to this gene × cell -->
<!--     species_list = list(unique(na.omit(species))), -->
<!--     n_species = length(unique(na.omit(species))), -->
<!--     .groups = "drop" -->
<!--   ) %>% -->
<!--   mutate( -->
<!--     per_site_table = pmap(list(all_pos, all_flags, all_int), function(pos, flag, int) { -->
<!--       if (length(pos) == 0) return(tibble(pos = numeric(0), flag = logical(0), int = numeric(0))) -->
<!--       tibble(pos = pos, -->
<!--              flag = ifelse(seq_along(pos) <= length(flag), flag, FALSE), -->
<!--              int  = ifelse(seq_along(pos) <= length(int), int, NA_real_)) -->
<!--     }), -->
<!--     per_site_by_pos = map(per_site_table, ~ { -->
<!--       df <- .x -->
<!--       if (nrow(df) == 0) return(tibble(pos = numeric(0), pos_flag_any = logical(0), pos_int = numeric(0))) -->
<!--       df %>% -->
<!--         group_by(pos) %>% -->
<!--         summarise( -->
<!--           pos_flag_any = any(flag, na.rm = TRUE), -->
<!--           pos_int = if (any(flag, na.rm = TRUE)) mean(int[flag], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_, -->
<!--           .groups = "drop" -->
<!--         ) -->
<!--     }), -->
<!--     n_pos_unique = map_int(per_site_by_pos, ~ sum(.x$pos_flag_any, na.rm = TRUE)), -->
<!--     prop_pos = n_pos_unique / seq_length, -->
<!--     mean_intensity_log10 = map_dbl(per_site_by_pos, function(df) { -->
<!--       df2 <- df %>% filter(pos_flag_any & !is.na(pos_int)) -->
<!--       if (nrow(df2) == 0) return(NA_real_) -->
<!--       mean(log10(df2$pos_int), na.rm = TRUE) -->
<!--     }), -->
<!--     max_intensity = map_dbl(per_site_by_pos, function(df) { -->
<!--       df2 <- df %>% filter(pos_flag_any & !is.na(pos_int)) -->
<!--       if (nrow(df2) == 0) return(NA_real_) -->
<!--       max(df2$pos_int, na.rm = TRUE) -->
<!--     }) -->
<!--   ) -->

<!-- pA -->
<!-- ####### PANEL D: -->
<!-- ##### ... take with a grain of salt, MEME is really supposed to work at the site level but  -->
<!-- ##### not necessarily the branch level... that's more resolution than can be relied on confidently for MEME -->
<!-- ##### there's more to be said about lineages and/or species paralogs where there is Consistent set of sites selected... but again take this with grain of salt as MEME is better at site lvl for gene than Branch level -->

<!-- TOP_N_SITES_PER_BRANCH <- 5 -->

<!--   unnest(c(pos_filtered, intensity_filtered)) %>% -->
<!--   rename( -->
<!--     pos = pos_filtered, -->
<!--     intensity_num = intensity_filtered -->
<!--   ) %>% -->
<!--   mutate(branch_f = factor(branch, levels = unique(branch))) -->

<!-- #CANCEL -->

<!-- ### HyPhy: RELAX Selection test Considers test branch(es) vs reference branch(es)... (macaca mulatta branches vs the rest of branches) -->
<!-- NOTE: this means when we do these plots we should be showing test branches relative to ref branches... granularity per branch is resolved no further than this for RELAX. -->

<!-- ```{r, RELAX} -->
<!-- #  -->
<!-- # relax_res <- read.csv("relax_harvest_consolidated.csv") -->
